commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

Add install.sh for one-line installation on macOS and Linux (#50)

## Summary

- Add `install.sh` to enable one-line installation without requiring Go
- Update `README.md` and `README.ja.md` with installation instructions

## What's Changed

### New Features
- **`install.sh`**: Shell script that auto-detects OS/arch, fetches the
latest release from GitHub API, and installs the binary to
`/usr/local/bin` (or a custom `INSTALL_DIR`)

### Usage

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nnstt1/hcpt/main/install.sh | sh
```

Custom install directory:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nnstt1/hcpt/main/install.sh | INSTALL_DIR=~/.local/bin sh
```

## Test plan
- [x] Run `bash install.sh` on macOS (arm64) — verified locally
- [ ] Run `bash install.sh` on macOS (amd64)
- [ ] Run `bash install.sh` on Linux (amd64)
- [ ] Run with `INSTALL_DIR=~/.local/bin` to test custom directory

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Harden all bot loops against spam

Territory reminders:
- Add in-memory dedup that survives file I/O failures
- Mark key before send so crash mid-send can't cause resend
- Wrap loop body in try/except so errors don't kill the loop

Alliance alerts:
- Add circuit breaker: skip cycle if >15 unsent alerts (likely bad data)
- Save state after each individual send instead of at end of cycle
  so partial failures don't resend already-sent alerts

Daily report:
- Save date marker BEFORE sending so send failures can't cause
  infinite retry spam every 5 minutes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

R22: Theorem Omega attack — 2-adic bridge, structured roots, verification extension, strategy synthesis

Four-agent investigation on closing the sole remaining gap:
- A (2-adic bridge): P_B(g)!=0 in Q_2 AND Q_3 (BOTH PROVED). CRT bridge IMPOSSIBLE
  (2-adic/3-adic worlds independent of mod-d). All p-adic obstructions exhaust at p|d.
- B (Structured roots): N_0(p) <= C * min(1, (k-1)/ord_p(2)) — structural bound.
  Coefficient divisibility chain c_j | c_{j+1} constrains root count.
- C (Verification extension): N_0=0 verified for k=3..17 via DP+CRT sieve.
  K_0=42 (tail<1). Gap = 24 values (k=18..41) between frontier and K_0.
- D (Theorem Omega): 6 strategies ranked. Top: structured root counting (30%).
  HONEST: unconditional proof probability 15-25% with known techniques.

Key new results:
- P_B(g) != 0 in Q_3 (NEW, extends 2-adic to 3-adic)
- Verification pushed to k=17 (was k=14)
- K_0 = 42 for Borel-Cantelli tail < 1

156/156 tests PASS, 6129 lines across 4 files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Promote score-tiered stops v3 to production default

Backtest-proven across 1Y and 3Y with no trade count impact:
  1Y: Sharpe +6.7%, PF +9.4%, MaxDD -3.3pp
  3Y: Sharpe +4.5%, PF +6.3%, MaxDD -12.3pp

Tiers: score≥85→1.25×ATR stop, 70-84→0.85×ATR, <70→0.50×ATR.
Strong signals get room to work; weak signals cut losses faster.

- Enable score_tiered_stops_enabled=True in config
- Apply score-tiered stops in live position evaluator (performance.py)
- Update experiment log verdict to pass

commit.message:
Restore maxZoom to 20 — maxNativeZoom:18 already prevents blue tiles

The blue tile fix is maxNativeZoom:18 (only fetches z18 tiles, upscales
for z19/z20). maxZoom just controls how far the user can zoom the map.
With offline tile caching + maxNativeZoom:18, z19/z20 work fine as
upscaled z18 tiles. Ski map stays at maxZoom:19.

null omission, type validation, and lifecycle transitions.

commit.message:
fix: use platform-specific scroll-lock fix for Android vs iOS (#670)

The touchstart boundary-nudge technique that fixes scroll-lock on iOS
Safari doesn't work on Android Chrome (different touch gesture model).
Instead, override overscroll-behavior to auto at runtime on Android.

Co-authored-by: Kristjan Elias <kristjanelias@Kairis-MacBook-Air.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[diaries] Use single query for generting summary (#4)

* Consolidate summary generation into single LLM query

Previously made one Haiku call per project, causing slow generation.
Now sends all projects in a single prompt and parses a keyed JSON response.
Also adds missing publish scripts to package.json.

* Update Diaries to v0.1.1

commit.message:
feat: v1.0.11 — domain/ 純粋型の抽出 (Clean Architecture Step 1)

- 新設 tmux_orchestrator/domain/ サブパッケージ (外部依存ゼロ):
  - domain/agent.py: AgentStatus, AgentRole (stdlib enum のみ)
  - domain/task.py: Task dataclass (stdlib dataclasses/secrets/time/typing のみ)
  - domain/message.py: MessageType, Message, BROADCAST (stdlib dataclasses/datetime/enum/uuid のみ)
  - domain/__init__.py: 全 public シンボルを re-export
- 後方互換シム: agents/base.py, config.py, bus.py で旧定義を削除し re-export に置換
  - 既存の全 import パスが変更なしで動作することを identity テストで検証
- tests/test_domain_purity.py (20 テスト):
  - AST 静的解析で domain/ が stdlib のみ import することを検証 (3 tests)
  - 各旧パスからのシム re-export が機能することを検証 (6 tests)
  - domain パッケージから直接 import できることを検証 (6 tests)
  - シムと domain の型が同一オブジェクトであることを検証 (5 tests)
- 全 1156 テスト通過 (既存 1136 + 新規 20)
- バージョン 1.0.10 → 1.0.11

Fix file system watcher assert by deferring init to event loop

The wxFileSystemWatcher inotify backend requires an active event loop,
but StartWatching() was called during construction before the loop started.
Use CallAfter to defer initialization.

commit.message:
Use SHA tags for yt-mcp image — enables Image Updater auto-rollouts

Image Updater can't detect changes when tag is "latest" since the tag
name doesn't change. Using SHA tags lets it compare and trigger updates.

commit.message:
fix: eliminate 255/260 hardcoded rgba() — full CSS variable compliance

Batch convert rgba() → color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-*) N%, transparent)
across 50 .tsx files (321 replacements via automated script + 6 manual fixes).

Remaining 5 rgba() are legitimate exceptions:
- FeatureTour: spotlight overlay (pure black dim)
- HelpTooltip: fixed dark tooltip bg (intentionally opaque)
- MonteCarloChart: dynamic opacity via template literals

Also converted 3 hardcoded hex colors in ImpliedVolatility IVGauge
to CSS variables (--color-warning/positive/info).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add ADR-009: dependency management policy + Dependabot config

Formalizes the rule that dependencies must be kept current before new
feature work begins. Covers:
- Pre-feature audit gate (npm audit --audit-level=high)
- Dedicated update PRs (never mixed with feature commits)
- Scheduled cadence: monthly patch/minor, quarterly major, Node LTS
- Dependabot weekly PRs with ecosystem grouping (vite, vitest, typescript)
- Major version upgrade process (one ecosystem per PR, full test suite)
- Node.js LTS alignment policy (target: Node 22)

Immediate action items listed in ADR: Node 22 base image, TS 5.9,
and evaluation PRs for React 19 / Vite 7 / Vitest 4.

CLAUDE.md updated with pre-feature audit requirement and ADR link.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Face Search: match unclustered faces in addition to cluster centroids

Previously Face Search only queried cluster centroids, so faces that
didn't meet MQ/LQ attach thresholds (cluster_id IS NULL) were invisible
despite having embeddings stored in the DB.

Changes:
- extraction_db: add load_unclustered_embeddings() returning face_id,
  image_path, bbox, magface_norm, and 512D embedding for all faces
  with cluster_id IS NULL
- face_search: search both cluster centroids AND unclustered embeddings,
  display unclustered matches in a separate section with "Reveal" button
  that uses `open -R` to highlight the source image in Finder
- Extracted _crop_face_thumbnail() helper to share crop logic between
  cluster and unclustered thumbnail generation
- Status bar shows breakdown: "3 clustered, 2 unclustered"

commit.message:
fix: canonicalize paths in FsWatcher and IgnoreRules for macOS compatibility

On macOS, TempDir creates paths under /var/folders/ but the canonical
path is /private/var/folders/. The ignore crate panics when event paths
don't fall under the gitignore root. Canonicalizing in both places
ensures consistent paths across platforms.
